title: Recursion
analogy: Recursion is like Russian nesting dolls — each doll contains a smaller version of itself. You keep opening until you reach the tiniest doll (base case), then work your way back out.

overview.summary: Recursion is a technique where a function calls itself with a smaller input, building solutions by combining results from sub-calls.
learningObjectives: Identify base cases and recursive cases.
Convert between iterative and recursive solutions.
overview.interviewRelevance: Recursion underpins tree traversals, divide-and-conquer, backtracking, and dynamic programming — all top interview categories.

paragraphs: File system directory listing (ls -R) is recursive — it lists files in a folder, then recurses into each subfolder.
Fractal art (Mandelbrot set, Sierpinski triangle) is generated by applying a recursive formula to each point.
realWorldHook.ahaMoment: Every recursive function trusts that a smaller version of itself will return the right answer — you just need to handle the base case and the combination step.

definition.technicalDefinition: Recursion is a method where a function solves a problem by reducing it to smaller instances of the same problem, with one or more base cases that terminate the recursion.

vocabulary.definition: The condition under which the function returns without making a recursive call.
The case where the function calls itself with a smaller or simpler input.
The stack of function calls maintained by the runtime. Each recursive call adds a frame.
Error when recursion goes too deep and exceeds the call stack limit.

keyProperties: Every recursive solution has at least one base case.
Each recursive call must make progress toward the base case.
Space complexity includes O(depth) for the call stack.

operations.name: Factorial
operations.visualNote: Call stack grows downward with each call, then results multiply back upward.

operations.name: Fibonacci
operations.description: fib(n) = fib(n-1) + fib(n-2); base cases: fib(0) = 0, fib(1) = 1.
operations.visualNote: Binary recursion tree showing overlapping subproblems (same fib(k) computed multiple times).

practice.title: Pow(x, n)
practice.keyInsight: Divide exponent by 2 each call: x^n = (x^(n/2))^2.

practice.title: Merge Sort
practice.keyInsight: Split in half, recursively sort, then merge.

practice.title: Generate Parentheses
practice.keyInsight: Recursive choice: add '(' if open < n, add ')' if close < open.

practice.title: Letter Combinations of a Phone Number
practice.keyInsight: Recurse over each digit, branching for each letter mapping.

conceptQuiz.question: What happens if a recursive function has no base case?
conceptQuiz.explanation: Without a base case, the function calls itself forever until the call stack overflows.

conceptQuiz.question: What is the space complexity overhead of recursion?
conceptQuiz.explanation: Each recursive call adds a frame to the call stack, so space is proportional to recursion depth.

recognitionQuiz.problemStatement: Compute the sum of all nodes in a binary tree.
recognitionQuiz.explanation: Tree problems naturally decompose into subtree problems — the hallmark of recursion.
